content.problemStatement:
Chef is given an array AAA consisting of NNN positive integers. Chef shuffles the array AAA and creates a new array BBB of length NNN, where Bi=(Ai+i) mod 2B_i = (A_i + i) \bmod 2Bi​=(Ai​+i)mod2, for each i  (1≤i≤N)i\;(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1≤i≤N).

Find the maximum possible sum of integers of the array BBB, if Chef shuffles the array AAA optimally.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the maximum sum of integers of the array BBB.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 3·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer – the maximum possible sum of the array B after an optimal shuffle of A.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: odds=2, evens=1, posOdd=2, posEven=1 → min(2,1)+min(1,2)=2.
Test case 2: odds=1, evens=2 → min(1,1)+min(2,2)=3.
Test case 3: odds=0, evens=2 → min(0,1)+min(2,1)=1.
All 5 numbers are odd. posOdd=3, posEven=2. Only odd numbers placed on even indices give 1. min(5,2)=2, min(0,3)=0 → answer 2.
All numbers are even. posOdd=2, posEven=2. Even numbers on odd indices give 1. min(0,2)=0, min(4,2)=2 → answer 2.

solution.approach: For each element only its parity matters because B_i = (A_i + i) mod 2 = (A_i mod 2) XOR (i mod 2). We want to place as many elements whose parity differs from the index parity as possible. Let o be the number of odd elements and e = N‑o the number of even elements. The number of odd indices is posOdd = (N+1)//2 and the number of even indices is posEven = N//2. An odd element placed on an even index contributes 1 to the sum, and an even element placed on an odd index also contributes 1. Hence the maximum sum is min(o, posEven) + min(e, posOdd). The algorithm simply counts odd elements, computes the two position counts, and applies the formula.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (single pass to count odds). The total over all test cases is O(ΣN) ≤ O(3·10^5).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional memory besides input storage.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    t = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(next(it))
        odd = 0
        for _ in range(n):
            x = int(next(it))
            if x & 1:
                odd += 1
        even = n - odd
        pos_odd = (n + 1) // 2
        pos_even = n // 2
        ans = min(odd, pos_even) + min(even, pos_odd)
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
